question: Disposition of asymmetric flagged contamination (one arm's model flagged on a task, the other clean): refuse the official render, or disclose-only?
author: claude/design

question: v1 memory-probe technique set: canary regurgitation + oracle-solution prefix continuation, canary-only, or additionally logprob/perplexity membership inference (vendor-gated: not all providers expose logprobs)?
author: claude/design

question: Solution-overlap metric: winnowing fingerprints (whitespace/rename robust) or n-gram Jaccard; threshold locked into the experiment at plan time either way?
author: claude/design

question: Corpus lifecycle of a contamination-flagged task: disclose-and-keep with a ledgered operator quarantine path (reusing flake-quarantine mechanics), or auto-quarantine on flag?
author: claude/design

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: all EVAL-10/11/12 recommendations accepted. Asymmetric flagged contamination invalidates the pairing itself — the one case A/B cannot disclose its way out of; exploratory still renders, watermarked, with the summary
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. Canary regurgitation is near-zero-false-positive proof of membership; oracle-prefix continuation covers solution leakage; logprob/perplexity inference is vendor-gated and statistically murkier — deferred to v2
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. Winnowing is robust to whitespace/rename cosmetics that defeat raw n-gram Jaccard; the threshold is locked into the experiment at plan time, never tuned post-hoc
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. A detector wrong in the auto-quarantine direction would silently shrink the corpus; disclosure plus a ledgered operator quarantine (flake-quarantine mechanics reused) keeps the human in the loop and the action auditable
author: jyang
